label: Meta Ads
description: Connect to Meta's Marketing API to read and manage Facebook and Instagram ad campaigns.

form.title: Connect Meta Ads
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to access your Facebook and Instagram ad accounts via the Meta Marketing API.

methods.label: OAuth via Meta App (BYOK)
methods.description: Connect using a Meta for Developers app you own. Provides secure, scoped access to ad account data via OAuth 2.0 — no passwords stored, revocable from Meta Business Settings at any time.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Meta OAuth (Bring Your Own App)

Meta requires you to create a Meta for Developers app to run the OAuth flow. This is a one-time setup.

## 1. Create a Meta App

1. Go to [Meta for Developers](https://developers.facebook.com/) and log in.
2. Click **My Apps → Create App**.
3. Select **Business** as the app type and click **Next**.
4. Give it a name (e.g., `Anton`) and select your Business Account, then click **Create App**.

## 2. Add the Marketing API Product

1. In your app dashboard, click **Add Product** and find **Marketing API**.
2. Click **Set Up** on the Marketing API card.
3. Under **Marketing API → Tools**, note that `ads_read`, `ads_management`, and `business_management` permissions are what Anton requires.

## 3. Configure OAuth Redirect

1. In the left sidebar go to **App Settings → Basic**.
2. Add `http://localhost` (and any local redirect port Anton uses) to the **App Domains** field.
3. Go to **Facebook Login → Settings** and add `http://localhost/callback` to **Valid OAuth Redirect URIs**.

## 4. Get Your App Credentials

1. Go to **App Settings → Basic**.
2. Copy your **App ID** (this is the Client ID) and click **Show** to reveal your **App Secret** (Client Secret).
3. Paste both into the fields below and click **Submit**.

## Notes

- Your app must be in **Live** mode (not Development) for other users to authorize it. For personal use, Development mode is fine.
- Tokens can be revoked in [Meta Business Integrations](https://www.facebook.com/settings?tab=business_tools).
- The Marketing API requires your ad account to be linked to a Business Manager.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: App ID (Client ID)
fields.description: Found in Meta for Developers → Your App → App Settings → Basic → App ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: App Secret (Client Secret)
fields.description: Found in App Settings → Basic → App Secret. Click 'Show' to reveal it.

fields.name: ad_account_id
fields.label: Ad Account ID
fields.description: Optional. The ad account to default to (format: act_XXXXXXXXXX). Found in Meta Ads Manager → Account Overview.

methods.label: System User Access Token
methods.description: Use a long-lived System User token generated from Meta Business Manager. Best for automated pipelines and server-to-server integrations where OAuth browser flows are impractical.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a System User Access Token

System User tokens are long-lived (60 days or non-expiring) and designed for server-side integrations. This method skips the browser OAuth flow entirely.

## Prerequisites

- You must be an admin of a **Meta Business Manager** account.
- A Meta app with the Marketing API product added (see the OAuth method for setup steps).

## Steps

1. Open [Meta Business Manager](https://business.facebook.com/) and go to **Business Settings**.
2. Under **Users**, click **System Users → Add**.
3. Name it (e.g., `Anton Integration`) and set the role to **Admin**.
4. Click **Generate New Token** next to your system user.
5. Select your **App** from the dropdown.
6. Enable the following permissions: `ads_read`, `ads_management`, `business_management`.
7. Click **Generate Token**. Copy the token immediately — it is shown only once.
8. Paste the token into the **Access Token** field below.

## Notes

- Set the token to **Never Expire** if you want a non-rotating credential; otherwise it expires in 60 days.
- The system user must be assigned to the relevant Ad Accounts in Business Settings → Accounts → Ad Accounts.
- If the token is compromised, revoke it in Business Settings → System Users and generate a new one.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: System User Access Token
fields.description: Long-lived access token generated from Meta Business Manager → System Users. Copy it immediately after generation — it is shown only once.

fields.name: ad_account_id
fields.label: Ad Account ID
fields.description: Optional. The ad account to default to (format: act_XXXXXXXXXX). Found in Meta Ads Manager → Account Overview.
